transportedTraceSpace.name: X_aug
transportedTraceSpace.definition: closure Ran(R_aug) with quotient norm inf{||f||_V:R_aug f=x}

moscoStatement.limsup: for every f in V there are finite core f_N with f_N -> f in graph norm and R_aug,N f_N -> R_aug f
moscoStatement.liminf: bounded finite graph-energy sequences have weak graph-form subsequential limits in V
moscoStatement.quotient: R_aug,N trace-image quotient norms converge to the quotient norm on X_aug
moscoStatement.lowerSemicontinuity: closed nonnegative finite repaired forms pass to the lower-semicontinuous envelope

coreDensityStatus.label: smooth/Galerkin core density in V
coreDensityStatus.reason: The Green closure theorem defines V as the graph-form closure of the smooth lifted core, giving the density input for recovery sequences.

moscoLimsupStatus.label: Mosco limsup recovery
moscoLimsupStatus.reason: Core density gives graph-form recovery sequences, and strong graph-dual trace convergence transports their R_aug,N coordinates to R_aug.

moscoLiminfStatus.label: Mosco liminf compactness
moscoLiminfStatus.reason: The completed Volterra graph norm is Hilbert.  Bounded graph-energy sequences therefore have weakly convergent subsequences, and closed graph forms are weakly lower semicontinuous.

traceQuotientCompatibilityStatus.label: trace quotient compatibility
traceQuotientCompatibilityStatus.reason: The transported quotient norm on X_aug is defined by R_aug, and strong graph-dual convergence identifies the finite trace quotients with the completed trace quotient.

closedFormLowerSemicontinuityStatus.label: closed-form lower semicontinuity
closedFormLowerSemicontinuityStatus.reason: Mosco limsup/liminf and quotient compatibility imply that nonnegative finite repaired forms have a nonnegative closed lower-semicontinuous envelope on V and X_aug.

proof: Use the Green closure theorem to define V as the graph-form closure of the smooth core.
Use augmented trace convergence for strong graph-dual convergence of R_aug,N.
Apply Mosco limsup recovery and liminf compactness in the Hilbert graph norm.
Use quotient norm compatibility to move the limit form to X_aug.
Apply lower semicontinuity to preserve nonnegativity in the closed form limit.
